pattern: Indexing each value's sorted positions lets a candidate found by random sampling be verified in logarithmic time via counting occurrences inside the queried range.
hints: Any element occurring in more than half a range is the strict majority, so at most one value can ever qualify per query.
If you pick a random position inside the range, a true majority element is the value you land on with probability above one half, so a few random probes almost surely surface the candidate.

Precompute, for every value, the sorted list of indices where it appears; then verifying a candidate's count in a range is two binary searches, and a handful of random samples per query finds the candidate cheaply.

edge_inputs.description: a clear majority, no majority, and a tight half-plus-one case
single element range meets a threshold of one
threshold exactly one above the true count returns minus one
two values tie so neither exceeds the half threshold
overlapping ranges with different dominant values

stress.description: random array with queries whose threshold respects the strict-majority bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 60)
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 6) for _ in range(n)]
    ops = ['MajorityChecker']
    args = [[arr]]
    q = max(1, min(size, 40))
    for _ in range(q):
        l = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        r = rng.randint(l, n - 1)
        span = r - l + 1
        lo = span // 2 + 1
        threshold = rng.randint(lo, span)
        ops.append('query'); args.append([l, r, threshold])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: threshold chosen so 2*threshold > span, matching the strict-majority guarantee
